name: Dumbbell Step-Up
resistance_profile: Single-leg, concentric-dominant knee extension driving up onto a box; hardest initiating from the bottom.
cues: Drive through the front heel, not the back foot
Stand tall at the top under control
Keep the front foot fully on the box
execution_steps: Stand facing a knee-height box or bench holding a dumbbell in each hand.
Place one whole foot flat on the box with the knee bent and the torso leaned slightly forward.
Drive through the front heel to stand fully on top of the box without pushing off the back foot.
Control the descent, lowering the trailing foot softly back to the floor.
Complete all reps on one leg or alternate, keeping the working foot planted on the box.
good_when: You want a single-leg, concentric-focused quad exercise
You want knee-friendly loading with an adjustable box height
bad_when: Balance limitations force you to cut the range short
You want a heavy bilateral quad overload
common_errors: Pushing off the trailing foot for momentum
Using too high a box so it becomes glute-dominant and loses quad tension
progressions: Increase the load or the box height
Add a pause at the top or slow the descent
regressions: Use bodyweight or hold a support for balance
